twitter_thread: Tested 27 'proper studio' gadgets for digital nomads. 20 were a waste of $1,200.
Rode Wireless Go II mic: 12-hour battery, -20dB noise reduction. Worth the $230.
Don't use your laptop's audio interface. Focusrite Scarlett is $110 and cuts noise by 90%.
Only 7% of digital nomads use noise-canceling software like Krisp. Fix that: it's $20/month.
sE Electronics Reflexion Filter: turns any café into a studio for $230, minus 50% echo.
Neewer 18-inch Outer Dimmable softbox: $60, and it makes a 30% difference in video quality.
What's your go-to gear for a 'proper studio' on the go? #digitalnomad #homestudio
pack.linkedin: After testing 27 'proper studio' gadgets for digital nomads, I found that 20 were a waste of $1,200. I also use Krisp noise-canceling software for $20/month. What's your experience with creating a 'proper studio' setup on the go?
